channel.title: ADMG 371 on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in ADMG 371 on RaihnForge

item.title: Informational Interview: Stakeholder Discovery as Career Research
item.description:
What Is an Informational Interview

Informational interviews are a tool that can help one increase understanding of career possibilities, gauge desire to pursue them, and raise awareness of possible employment (FiskeJul, 2008). They allow the interviewee to learn more about the industry, the organization, and possible employment opportunities that may be of interest (Knight, 2016). The primary objective is to deeply understand the realities of a particular occupation (Knight, 2016). That understanding allows one to orient self-assessment tools such as SWOT, SMART goals, and personal development planning in a focused way that is more directly aimed at achieving a specific career goal.

item.title: Management Theories: Scientific, Administrative, Behavioral, and the Contingency Frame
item.description:
TO: Dr. Susan Rivera FROM: Joshua Keyes DATE: April 08, 2021 SUBJECT: M2A, Management Theories

Contingency Theories

Contingency theories are anchored by the concept of a leader’s use of different leadership styles as situations change. Situational leadership implies that success can be achieved when the leadership style matches the situation. There is contention between developers of the theory. Ken Blanchard and Paul Hersey differ from Fred Fiedler. Fiedler believes that personalities have innate leadership styles (VanHuss, 2014, p. 35). Fiedler would conclude that if the situation changes, a new person would need to be found to fit the leadership style required by the situation. In opposition, Blanchard and Hersey’s concept essentially credits individuals with a greater ability to be flexible. Their view is that the same person could adapt to a new required leadership style.
